title: Hi explain the architecture of this service ? and this runs

messages.content: Hi explain the architecture of this service ? and this runs on which port ?

Architecture: A workspace-aware business assistant gateway for customer/admin/agent flows with explicit boundaries between routing, policy, and execution.


Main runtime path: sanitize → build workspace/customer context → route intent → apply policy → backend/tool execution → guard response


Core modules:


Workspace/config: core/workspace.js, setup/profileService.js


Customer runtime: runtime/customerFlow.js, runtime/agentChain.js


Routing: gateway/customerRouter.js, gateway/intentParser.js


Policy/governance: runtime/customerPolicy.js, gateway/policyEngine.js


Backend conversational context: runtime/customerContext.js




How routing works: Heuristics first, then LLM classification if needed, constrained by manifest-defined intents.


Entry point: index.js


Requires: node index.js --agent <manifest.yml>


Transport options: whatsapp (default), http, cli




Port: Not defined in index.js or package.json.


Likely configured inside:


transport/api.js


transports/http.js






Most likely behavior: HTTP console/API starts when using whatsapp transport.

check all files to find out how openclaw is integrated with it.

Answer: OpenClaw is integrated as a selectable backend conversation path, not as the primary router/policy engine.


Where it plugs in: runtime/customerFlow.js


Imports:


getFlowConfig from providers/llm


decideCustomerExecution / getCustomerExecutionConfig from flowOrchestrator




This implies runtime decides whether a customer turn goes to tool flow vs backend flow (e.g. OpenClaw).




What OpenClaw receives: runtime/customerContext.js


buildCustomerBackendMessages(...) assembles the backend payload with:


system prompt


business profile facts


customer profile memory


DB context + schema


notes


RAG hints


conversation history/state


resolved request


policy block context






How it’s framed: prompt says:


“If the configured mode is a backend service, keep this request on that backend path.”




Conclusion: OpenClaw is likely configured through LLM/backend provider settings and used after routing + policy, as the governed conversational backend.
